# Promote v13 release identity above Agent OS maturity

## Context

Operator correction on 2026-06-07 surfaced a public-framing gap in the merged v13 release-note checkpoint: `resources/content/release-notes/v13.0.0.md` currently opens with:

```md
**Release Type:** MX Loop and Agent OS Maturity
```

That line is technically true for one subsystem arc, but it undersells the release described by the artifact itself. The same file immediately says "v13 is the release where Neo learned to maintain itself," and the parent epic frames v13 as the release where the cross-family swarm, Memory Core, Native Edge Graph, Dream/Golden Path, Orchestrator, skills, cloud deployment, harness recovery, and Body continuity became an operating substrate.

This issue is a narrow child of #12696. It exists because #12695 is closed via PR `#12697`; release-note iteration must continue through follow-up subs, not by pretending the merged checkpoint was final.

Pre-creation evidence on 2026-06-07:

- Live latest-open sweep: checked latest 20 open issues with number, title, author, labels, and URL. Found #12700, #12698, #12696, and other v13/MX items, but no open ticket owning the top-level v13 release identity or `Release Type` correction.
- Exact live sweep: `v13 release identity OR release type OR Neo Learned to Maintain Itself OR organism release` found #12698 and #12696, but no equivalent issue for the release identity row.
- Exact local sweep across `README.md`, `learn/benefits/Introduction.md`, `resources/content/issues`, `resources/content/discussions`, `resources/content/release-notes`, and `.agents/skills/neo-identity-update` found the canonical organism apex and the current `Release Type` row, but no active leaf ticket for this correction.
- Semantic KB ticket sweep for `ticket v13 release identity Release Type Neo Learned to Maintain Itself organism release notes` found no matching ticket.
- Identity source-of-authority checked: README opening and `neo-identity-update` framing governance define the apex as `Neo.mjs is a self-evolving software organism`, with Body and Brain as the two hemispheres beneath it. Discussion `#10137` verifies MX as the evolution loop, not the whole release label. Discussion `#10119` verifies the Body/Neural-Link inhabitation substrate, so Body cannot be demoted to a footnote.
- Project pre-flight: attach to Project 12 (`Neo v13 Release`) per ticket-create release-cycle rule.

## The Problem

The current release-note metadata makes the release sound smaller than the evidence. `MX Loop and Agent OS Maturity` reads like an internal Agent OS maturity release. v13 is larger: a 1,300-PR-class organism threshold where Body, Brain, Institution/Swarm, and Evolution/MX become mutually visible.

This matters because the `Release Type` row is the first public classification after the title. A narrow subsystem phrase there trains readers to treat the rest of the artifact as internal process notes. The artifact needs the opposite: a release identity that carries the organism-level transformation, while still leaving MX / Agent OS maturity as a major chapter inside the release.

## The Architectural Reality

The current artifact already contains the stronger framing:

- `resources/content/release-notes/v13.0.0.md` line 14: "v13 is the release where Neo learned to maintain itself."
- `resources/content/release-notes/v13.0.0.md` line 20: `## Neo Learned to Maintain Itself`.
- README opening: `Neo.mjs is a self-evolving software organism - a professional, end-to-end AI engineering team that lives in its own open-source repository.`
- `neo-identity-update` framing governance: the canonical apex is self-evolving software organism; Body and Brain are the two hemispheres beneath it; Neural Link and MX are mechanisms/capabilities, not the apex.
- Discussion `#10137`: MX is the inward-facing friction-to-substrate evolution loop. It is load-bearing but not the whole release category.
- Discussion `#10119`: the Body / Neural Link / runtime inhabitation substrate is part of why the Brain can exist; the release identity must not read as a pivot away from the engine.

The correction should therefore promote an organism-level release identity at the top and demote `MX Loop and Agent OS Maturity` to a chapter/theme formulation.

## The Fix

Update the v13 release-note artifact so the top-level release identity matches the release's actual scope.

Expected prescription:

- Replace the current `Release Type` value with an organism-level phrase, likely one of:
  - `Self-Evolving Software Organism Release`
  - `Neo Learned to Maintain Itself`
  - `Body + Brain + Institution Convergence`
  - another operator-approved phrase that carries the same scope.
- Keep `MX Loop` and `Agent OS maturity` in the prose as internal mechanisms / chapter themes, not as the top-level release type.
- Tighten any adjacent sentence that still says "The headline is Agent OS maturity" so it does not contradict the new release identity.
- Do not rewrite every identity surface in this issue. This is the release-note artifact projection of the existing ADR 0018 / README apex, not a new global identity change.

## Contract Ledger Matrix

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `resources/content/release-notes/v13.0.0.md` `Release Type` row | README apex, ADR 0018 via `neo-identity-update`, parent epic #12696, operator correction | Classify v13 at organism level rather than subsystem maturity level | If final wording is not settled, use a clearly stronger temporary release type and leave an explicit release-cut checklist item | Release-note artifact | Diff shows line 10 no longer says `MX Loop and Agent OS Maturity` |
| First-section headline/prose | Current release note and parent epic #12696 | Make `Neo Learned to Maintain Itself` or equivalent the release identity, with Agent OS/MX as mechanisms underneath | Keep current section order but adjust the contradiction sentence only | Release-note artifact | Grep confirms no remaining top-level claim that the headline is merely Agent OS maturity |
| Public identity compatibility | `README.md`, `neo-identity-update` framing governance, Discussions `#10119` and `#10137` | Release-note framing remains compatible with self-evolving software organism, Body/Brain hemispheres, and MX loop | Escalate to operator if a proposed phrase changes the global apex rather than projecting it | PR body | Review cites identity-source checks and no client/private claims |

## Decision Record impact

Aligned with ADR 0018 / `neo-identity-update` framing governance. This ticket does not amend ADR 0018; it applies the existing organism apex to the v13 release-note artifact.

## Discussion Criteria Mapping

| Source criterion | Ticket acceptance mapping |
|---|---|
| Parent epic #12696: v13 release is an epic, not a single final artifact | This issue becomes one narrow follow-up child after #12695 closed. |
| Discussion #12694: Agent OS maturity must read as evolution, not framework abandonment | AC1-AC3 promote the organism-level identity while preserving Body/Brain bridge language. |
| Operator correction: `MX Loop and Agent OS Maturity` undersells 1,300 merged PRs | AC1 directly removes the underselling top-level release type. |
| ADR 0018 / README apex | AC2-AC4 require compatibility with self-evolving software organism framing. |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] AC1: `resources/content/release-notes/v13.0.0.md` no longer uses `MX Loop and Agent OS Maturity` as the top-level `Release Type`.
- [ ] AC2: The replacement release type or title row communicates the organism-level v13 scope: Body + Brain + Institution/Swarm + Evolution/MX, not just Agent OS internals.
- [ ] AC3: Adjacent prose no longer says or implies that the v13 headline is merely `Agent OS maturity`; Agent OS and MX remain framed as load-bearing mechanisms inside the wider release identity.
- [ ] AC4: The wording stays compatible with README / ADR 0018 `self-evolving software organism` framing and does not introduce a new global identity apex.
- [ ] AC5: Public-surface hygiene is checked: no client names, private deployment vehicles, private strategy, or unverifiable market superlatives.
- [ ] AC6: The PR body records the identity-source checks used for the wording choice and requests cross-family review before merge.

## Out of Scope

- Rewriting the full v13 release note.
- Completing #12698 war-story mining.
- Completing #12700 Memory Core / telepathy chapter work.
- Creating the future release-notes skill #10321.
- Updating README, package metadata, SEO generation, GitHub repo metadata, or ADR 0018.
- Publishing the v13 release or changing package versions.

## Avoided Traps

- Rejected: leaving the technically-correct subsystem phrase at the top. It is accurate locally but wrong at release scale.
- Rejected: using `MX Loop` as the whole release category. MX is the evolution mechanism; the release is the organism threshold it enabled.
- Rejected: using `Agent OS maturity` alone. It risks reading as a pivot away from the Body, contradicting the Body/Brain bridge and #10119 inhabitation substrate.
- Rejected: broad identity rewrite. The current problem is one release-note projection of the existing apex, not a new identity doctrine.
- Rejected: closing #12696 after #12695. Final means the epic is resolved, not that one checkpoint PR landed.

### @neo-gpt - 2026-06-07T22:44:04Z

Operator input captured for the implementation pass:

The release identity correction should explicitly account for the v13 **graph cognition** layer, not just generic Agent OS maturity.

Verified evidence from the cited substrates:

- `README.md` already frames the Brain around the Native Edge Graph and DreamService: intelligence lives in the Native Edge Graph, distilled by `DreamService` into Golden Path topology, and "the graph routes us."
- `learn/agentos/DreamPipeline.md` documents the shipped pipeline: workspace ingestion into the Native Edge Graph, Tri-Vector extraction, SQLite `GraphService.upsertNode()` / `linkNodes()`, topological conflict detection, deterministic concept-gap inference, vector apoptosis, semantic + structural Golden Path scoring, and `sandman_handoff.md` output.
- `ai/graph/` contains the Native Edge Graph database/model/storage/query layer; `ai/services/graph/` contains `SemanticGraphExtractor`, `TopologyInferenceEngine`, `GapInferenceEngine`, `GoldenPathSynthesizer`, `GraphMaintenanceService`, provider dispatch/readiness, and lazy edge drainage.
- `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/config.template.mjs` defaults the memory engine to `hybrid` (Chroma vectors + SQLite graph) and points the handoff output at `resources/content/sandman_handoff.md`.
- `ai/config.template.mjs` separates graph-generation from generic chat: `graphProvider` defaults to `openAiCompatible`; `openAiCompatible.model` defaults to `gemma-4-31b-it`; native Ollama defaults to `gemma4:31b`. The `31b` suffix is load-bearing: do not collapse this to generic "Gemma4" in the release prose, because the point is the high-capacity 31B local graph-processing route rather than a smaller Gemma4 variant. If final public prose calls 31B the "max" Gemma4 size, run an external model-family V-B-A at release cut; the repo-local claim already supported here is that Neo's configured graph-processing route names the 31B variant.
- `resources/content/sandman_handoff.md` is a concrete generated artifact: it currently shows Native Edge Graph audit output, concept gaps/reverification queues, active PR cycle state, and a computed Golden Path strategic recommendation.

Implementation implication for #12702:

The stronger top-level release identity is not only "Neo learned to maintain itself" in a social/process sense. It is also mechanical: v13 created a hybrid vector + graph cognition layer where 31B local-model graph extraction feeds a SQLite Native Edge Graph, and `mutate_frontier` / Golden Path style routing can actively steer the next work. `MX Loop and Agent OS Maturity` should be demoted because it hides this deeper mechanism.
